# unicode-spinners

Over 150 incredibly lightweight, zero-dependency terminal and web loading animations provided as raw string arrays.

## Core API

### `spinners`
The default export is an object containing all animations.
Each spinner follows the `Spinner` interface:
```typescript
interface Spinner {
  readonly frames: readonly string[];
  readonly interval: number; // Suggested interval in ms
  readonly category?: string;
}
```

Example usage in Node.js:
```typescript
import spinners from 'unicode-spinners';

const { frames, interval } = spinners.earth;
let i = 0;
const timer = setInterval(() => {
  // \r returns to start of line, \x1B[2K clears the line
  process.stdout.write(`\r\x1B[2K  ${frames[i++ % frames.length]} Loading...`);
}, interval);
```

### CLI
The library also exposes a CLI tool for agents and humans to wrap long-running commands.
```bash
npx unicode-spinners wrap --spinner pacman --text "Downloading..." -- curl -O http://example.com/file
```

### Advanced: Custom Braille
You can generate custom braille spinners using the included utilities:
```typescript
import { makeGrid, gridToBraille } from 'unicode-spinners';
const grid = makeGrid(4, 2);
grid[0][0] = true;
console.log(gridToBraille(grid));
```
